findings.evidence: `rg` confirms existing closure lemmas at `Basic.lean:229`, `Basic.lean:238`, `Basic.lean:314`, and `Basic.lean:324`; the new namespace starting at `Basic.lean:356` contains only `continuousOn`, `isCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi`, `of_isCompletelyMonotone`, `antitoneOn_Ioi`, `nonneg*`, `le_zero`, `shift_pos`, and `congr` through line 467.
findings.fix: Add `IsClosedCompletelyMonotone.add` and `IsClosedCompletelyMonotone.smul` (and finite-sum variants if following the existing local pattern), proved from `ContinuousOn.add`/`.const_smul` and the existing `IsCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi.add`/`.smul`.
findings.issue: `IsClosedCompletelyMonotone` exposes only projections/congruence and pointwise consequences, but no `add` or nonnegative `smul` closure lemmas, unlike `IsCompletelyMonotone` and `IsCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi` in the same file; this makes the new roadmap-level predicate incomplete for the operations already in scope.

summary: The new closed-half-line predicate is exposed without the basic closure API already available for the adjacent completely-monotone predicates, so downstream users cannot work with the roadmap-level object without dropping back to its fields.
